_meta.status: SEED — illustrative library shapes, NOT confirmed from real captured workflows.
_meta.provenance_warning: Every entry is confirmed:false on purpose. These are realistic, format-correct mappings whose only job is to let the library-match mechanism (recipeUnitSource) resolve and rank a fitting recipe for a captured unit. They were authored, not derived from a real confirm-pass over actual captured-and-confirmed workflows. BEFORE ANY PILOT: replace this file with the output of a real confirm-pass, and only then set confirmed:true. Do NOT present these to a client as confirmed/trusted recipes — that would misrepresent provenance, which the product forbids.
_meta.framing: All recipe text is leverage / assist-not-replace. A human-held unit keeps the person approving and wears the reserved Human Pink. The economics-token family is excluded by construction.
_meta.match_vocabulary: The match signatures key off the SAME shape inputs the Leverage Map detector already reads — they are NOT a parallel vocabulary. For a connection/span unit: 'motivators' is a subset of the detector's seam motivator kinds (shared-tool | manual-channel | system-switching | transition), 'handoff_kind' is one of HANDOFF_KINDS (role-to-role | human-to-system | system-to-human | system-to-system) and requires a 'transition' motivator. For a step unit: 'step_type' is one of STEP_TYPE_OPTIONS, 'friction' of FRICTION_KINDS, 'role' of ROLE_VALUES, 'decision' of DECISION_KINDS. 'human_held' mirrors the seam/step human-hold (held recipes fit held units only). 'tools_any' is a SOFT disambiguator (token overlap with the captured tool text); it never gates a fit, only ranks.

schema.id: stable identifier for the seed entry
schema.label: human-readable name of the workflow shape
schema.unit_kind: step | connection | span (Option A — a recipe unit is a step, a handoff, or a short span; a span entry fits a connection unit)
schema.match: the captured-shape signature, in the detector's real vocabulary, the matcher compares a unit against
schema.leverage: lo | md | hi — the rank used to order fitting recipes (never a percent, never an economics number)
schema.human_held: true if the unit involves judgment a person must own; the recipe then keeps the person approving and wears reserved Human Pink (#FF4FD8)
schema.source: always 'example' in this file; becomes 'library' (confirmed) only after a real confirm-pass

entries.label: Export → manual reconciliation (controller close)
entries.recipe: AI assembles the reconciliation working file from both sources and surfaces the items that do not tie out, so the person reviews the exceptions instead of building the comparison by hand.

entries.label: Spreadsheet → ledger re-key (accounts payable)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the ledger entries from the source export and flags mismatches for review, so the re-keying between the two systems is carried for the person rather than done by hand.

entries.label: Invoice routed for approval (accounts payable)
entries.recipe: AI packages the invoice with its supporting detail and a recommended coding and routes it to the approver; the person approves. Never removes the approval step.

entries.label: Package the variance summary for review (controller close)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the variance summary from the reconciled figures, groups the items for review, and notes what moved, so the person spends their attention on which variances matter.

entries.label: Gather forecast inputs into the model (FP&A)
entries.recipe: AI gathers the forecast inputs from the source systems and assembles them into the model's input shape, so the person works on the assumptions rather than the collection.

entries.label: Flag overdue → collections outreach (accounts receivable)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the collections outreach for each overdue item and prepares it for sending, so the person reviews and sends rather than composing each message from scratch.

entries.label: Assemble the daily cash position (treasury)
entries.recipe: AI assembles the daily cash position from the account feeds and highlights anything unusual, so the person reviews a prepared position instead of compiling it.

entries.label: Assess a control (SOX compliance)
entries.recipe: AI assembles and routes the control evidence with a draft assessment for review; the person makes the control assessment. Never removes the human assessment.

entries.label: Export → cross-check for stale deals (revenue operations)
entries.recipe: AI assembles the week-over-week comparison and proposes which deals look stale with the supporting signals; the person decides what counts as stale. Never removes the judgment.

entries.label: Stale deals → owner messaging and leadership summary (revenue operations)
entries.recipe: AI drafts owner outreach for each flagged deal and the leadership summary and prepares them for sending, so the person reviews and routes rather than writing each one.

entries.label: Intake request → route to category (procurement)
entries.recipe: AI drafts the intake summary and proposes the routing category from the request detail, so the person confirms and forwards rather than triaging from scratch.

entries.label: Route an item for sign-off (general)
entries.recipe: AI packages the item with its supporting detail and routes it to the right approver with a recommended decision; the person approves. Never removes the sign-off.
